title: Project Metadata, Resources, PyInstaller, and Platform Builds · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: An internal desktop software release engineer. They need a pure core, safe versioned replay, keyboard-first and multichannel puzzle interface, property and mutation evidence, representative profiles, platform-native PyInstaller builds, and clean create-solve-save-recover-close launch evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Tested Cross-Platform Release Candidate with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Keep topology, generation, search, state transitions, and evidence free of Tk widget and operating-system dependencies.
Validate version, types, bounds, enums, lengths, unknown fields, and cross-field invariants before constructing state.
Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, scaling, contrast, screen-reader, switch-like input, and disability-led playtest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Domain Core
steps.instruction:
project case — A project puzzle team handles case 7515 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile package metadata, resources, Tcl and Tk assets, native artifacts, and clean-install launches; the prior good state must survive failure; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Plan the evidence sequence · Project Domain Core, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Domain Core precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Clean Install Smoke
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-clean-install-smoke-12t0t9" that uses case 5453 to reconcile package metadata, resources, Tcl and Tk assets, native artifacts, and clean-install launches. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Clean Install Smoke.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Clean Install Smoke into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Clean Install Smoke, keep Clean Install Smoke connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-clean-install-smoke-12t0t9
# Competency: maze-clean-install-smoke.
# Case 5453: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: event-loop work must remain cancellable.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_clean_install_smoke_12t0t9(target_platforms: int = 3, native_builds: int = 3, clean_launches: int = 3, missing_tk_assets: int = 0, artifact_digests: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_12t0t9 = "12t0t9-9t0t21-5453"
    assert evidence_variant_12t0t9
    evidence_axes_12t0t9 = (
        "grid12t0t9",
        "topology5453",
        "search9t0t21",
        "event12t0t95453",
        "access545312t0t9",
        "recovery9t0t215453",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_12t0t9)) == 6
    if target_platforms < 1 or native_builds < 0:
        return False, native_builds, "platform-matrix"
    if native_builds != target_platforms or clean_launches != target_platforms:
        return False, clean_launches, "clean-install-gap"
    if missing_tk_assets > 0:
        return False, missing_tk_assets, "tk-resource-gap"
    if artifact_digests != target_platforms:
        return False, artifact_digests, "artifact-identity"
    assert clean_launches == target_platforms
    return True, clean_launches, "platform-native-release"
